definition: A structured set of data associated with a specific product, accessible digitally throughout its entire lifecycle, designed to enhance transparency, improve product traceability, and facilitate the transition towards a circular economy.

EU Regulation (EU) 2024/1781, which mandates the Digital Product Passport and sets ecodesign requirements for nearly all physical goods placed on the EU market to improve their environmental sustainability.

An economic system aimed at eliminating waste and the continual use of resources. Circular systems employ reuse, repair, refurbishment, remanufacturing, and recycling to create a closed-loop system, minimizing resource input and waste generation.

A standard that extends the utility of traditional GS1 identifiers by making them web-enabled, allowing a product's unique identifier to link directly to online information, including its Digital Product Passport.
The physical or digital mechanism (e.g., QR code, RFID tag, NFC chip) that links a tangible product to its Digital Product Passport.
The standardized structure and format for organizing the information contained within a Digital Product Passport, ensuring interoperability across different products and sectors.
A central or distributed system for storing, managing, and providing access to Digital Product Passports, ensuring data integrity and access control.
The total greenhouse gas emissions caused by an individual, event, organization, service, place or product, expressed as carbon dioxide equivalent.
A serverless SQL database built on SQLite, distributed globally across Cloudflare's edge network, used for storing structured, relational data with low latency.
A globally distributed, eventually consistent key-value store optimized for high-read workloads, used for storing frequently accessed, less structured data or for caching.
A cryptographic hash function used for data integrity and security, generating unique, fixed-size hash values for data to verify authenticity and detect tampering.
An EU initiative designed to put a fair price on the carbon emitted during the production of carbon-intensive goods that are imported into the EU, supported by verifiable data from DPPs.
South African legislation that governs the processing of personal information, ensuring data privacy and protection.
The South African government agency responsible for business registration and compliance, as well as intellectual property rights.
